Distribution Family and Evaluation Metrics

Appendix_Distr.Rmd

Distribution family

BRIER2 supports a wide range of distribution families for modeling complex traits, including continuous, binary, count, and survival outcomes, as summarized in Table @ref(tab:family). For each distribution family, the corresponding evaluation metrics used to assess predictive performance are provided in Table @ref(tab:metrics) (see Section 10). In BRIER2, the model family is specified through the family argument in the model fitting functions BRIERfull, BRIERi, and BRIERs, with "gaussian" used as the default. In the following sections, we present step-by-step tutorials using Gaussian outcomes as an illustrative example; guidance for other distribution families is provided in Modeling with Complex Distributions.

(#tab:family) Distribution families implemented in BRIER2 and the corresponding model specification.
Outcome Type Family Family Argument \(g(\mu_i)\) / model specification
Continuous Gaussian gaussian \(\mu_i = \boldsymbol{x}_i^\top \boldsymbol{\beta}\)
Case-control Binomial binary \(\log\!\left(\dfrac{\mu_i}{1-\mu_i}\right) = \boldsymbol{x}_i^\top \boldsymbol{\beta}\)
Count Poisson poisson \(\log(\mu_i) = \boldsymbol{x}_i^\top \boldsymbol{\beta}\)
Count Negative Binomial negbin \(\log\!\left(\dfrac{\mu_i}{\mu_i+r}\right) = \boldsymbol{x}_i^\top \boldsymbol{\beta}\)
Time-to-event Cox Proportional Hazards survival \(\lambda_i(t)=\lambda_0(t)\exp(\boldsymbol{x}_i^\top \boldsymbol{\beta})\)

Evaluation of BRIER2 model

We provide comprehensive metrics to evaluate fitted BRIER2 predictive performance on validation or testing datasets, as summarized in Table @ref(tab:metrics).

(#tab:metrics) Evaluation metrics for outcome families implemented in BRIER2. Abbreviations: MSPE, mean squared prediction error; PD, predicted deviance; AUC, area under the ROC curve.
Family Metric Criteria Argument Formula Optimal
Continuous MSPE (PD) gaussian.mspe \(\dfrac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n (y_i-\widehat{y}_i)^2\) Min
Continuous Pearson \(R^2\) gaussian.pearson.rsq \(\dfrac{\left\{\sum_{i=1}^n (y_i-\bar{y})(\widehat{y}_i-\overline{\widehat{y}})\right\}^2}{\left\{\sum_{i=1}^n (y_i-\bar{y})^2\right\}\left\{\sum_{i=1}^n (\widehat{y}_i-\overline{\widehat{y}})^2\right\}}\) Max
Binary PD binomial.dev \(-2\sum_{i=1}^n\left[y_i\log(\widehat{y}_i)+(1-y_i)\log(1-\widehat{y}_i)\right]\) Min
Binary Tjur \(R^2\) binomial.Tjur.rsq \(\dfrac{1}{n_1}\sum_{i:y_i=1}\widehat{y}_i-\dfrac{1}{n_0}\sum_{i:y_i=0}\widehat{y}_i\) Max
Binary AUC binomial.AUC \(\mathrm{Pr}(\widehat{y}_i>\widehat{y}_j \mid y_i=1,\; y_j=0)\) Max
Count PD poisson.dev \(2\sum_{i=1}^n\left[y_i\log\!\left(\dfrac{y_i}{\widehat{y}_i}\right)-(y_i-\widehat{y}_i)\right]\) Min
Count PD negbin.dev \(2\sum_{i=1}^n\left[y_i\log\!\left(\dfrac{y_i}{\widehat{y}_i}\right)-(y_i+r)\log\!\left(\dfrac{y_i+r}{\widehat{y}_i+r}\right)\right]\) Min
Survival PD survival.dev \(-2\sum_{i\in\mathrm{E}}\left[\log(\widehat{r}_i)-\log\!\left(\sum_{j\in\mathrm{R}_i}\widehat{r}_j\right)\right]\) Min
Survival C-index survival.Cindex \(\mathrm{Pr}(\widehat{r}_i>\widehat{r}_j \mid T_i<T_j)\) Max

Here \(y_i\) denotes the observed outcome, \(\widehat{y}_i\) the predicted mean (or risk), and \(\widehat{r}_i=\exp(\boldsymbol{x}_i^\top\boldsymbol{\beta})\) the predicted relative risk for survival outcomes. For binary outcomes, \(n_1\) and \(n_0\) denote the numbers of cases and controls, respectively. For negative binomial outcomes, \(r\) denotes the dispersion parameter. For survival outcomes, \(\mathrm{E}\) denotes individuals with observed events and \(\mathrm{R}_i\) the corresponding risk set.

On this page
